findings.description: Potential hardcoded credential detected in variable assignment. Store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager.
findings.remediation: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
findings.what_is_wrong: Credentials or tokens are embedded in source code.
findings.why_dangerous: Secrets in repos are scraped by bots and abused at scale.
findings.real_world_impact: Account takeover, cloud resource abuse, compliance violations.

findings.description: Hardcoded credential or secret in source.
findings.remediation: Load secrets from environment or secret manager.
findings.what_is_wrong: Credentials or tokens are embedded in source code.
findings.why_dangerous: Secrets in repos are scraped by bots and abused at scale.
findings.real_world_impact: Account takeover, cloud resource abuse, compliance violations.

findings.description: OS command execution with potential user-controlled input.
findings.remediation: Avoid shell=True; use argument lists; validate allowlists.
findings.what_is_wrong: Shell commands include unsanitized user input.
findings.why_dangerous: Attackers execute arbitrary OS commands on the server.
findings.real_world_impact: Remote code execution, ransomware deployment, lateral movement.

findings.description: os.system() executes a shell command. If the argument is user-controlled, this leads to command injection (CWE-78). Use subprocess with shell=False instead.
findings.remediation: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
findings.what_is_wrong: Shell commands include unsanitized user input.
findings.why_dangerous: Attackers execute arbitrary OS commands on the server.
findings.real_world_impact: Remote code execution, ransomware deployment, lateral movement.

findings.remediation: Use yaml.safe_load; never unpickle untrusted bytes.
findings.why_dangerous: Gadget chains can lead to arbitrary code execution.
findings.real_world_impact: Full server compromise via malicious payloads.

findings.description: Deserializing untrusted data with pickle can lead to arbitrary code execution. Use safer alternatives like json.
findings.remediation: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
findings.why_dangerous: Gadget chains can lead to arbitrary code execution.
findings.real_world_impact: Full server compromise via malicious payloads.

findings.description: MD5 is a cryptographically weak hash function. Use SHA-256 or stronger algorithms for security-sensitive operations.
findings.remediation: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
findings.what_is_wrong: Deprecated or broken crypto primitives are used.
findings.why_dangerous: Ciphertext can be broken with modern attacks.
findings.real_world_impact: Decryption of sensitive data, forged signatures.

findings.remediation: Use AES-GCM, SHA-256+, and modern libraries.
findings.what_is_wrong: Deprecated or broken crypto primitives are used.
findings.why_dangerous: Ciphertext can be broken with modern attacks.
findings.real_world_impact: Decryption of sensitive data, forged signatures.

findings.remediation: Use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords; SHA-256+ for integrity.
findings.what_is_wrong: A security anti-pattern was detected in this code region.
findings.why_dangerous: It may violate secure coding standards and enable exploitation.
findings.real_world_impact: Impact depends on context; treat as security debt until reviewed.
findings.secure_example: # Apply principle of least privilege and input validation

findings.remediation: Remove eval/exec; use safe parsers or explicit logic.
findings.what_is_wrong: Dynamic execution of code from strings (eval/exec).
findings.why_dangerous: Any input can become executable attacker code.
findings.secure_example: # Use ast.literal_eval only for literals, or structured parsers

findings.description: eval() executes arbitrary Python expressions. If the argument is user-controlled, this leads to code injection.
findings.remediation: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
findings.what_is_wrong: Dynamic execution of code from strings (eval/exec).
findings.why_dangerous: Any input can become executable attacker code.
findings.secure_example: # Use ast.literal_eval only for literals, or structured parsers

summary: Analysis complete: risk score 100/100 (CRITICAL). Found 13 issue(s) across 6 categories. ML suspicious probability: 99%.
recommendations: [HIGH] HARDCODED_SECRET: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
[CRITICAL] COMMAND_INJECTION: Avoid shell=True; use argument lists; validate allowlists.
[CRITICAL] UNSAFE_DESERIALIZATION: Use yaml.safe_load; never unpickle untrusted bytes.
[HIGH] WEAK_CRYPTOGRAPHY: Follow Semgrep rule guidance; apply secure coding pattern.
[MEDIUM] WEAK_HASHING: Use bcrypt/argon2 for passwords; SHA-256+ for integrity.
[CRITICAL] CODE_INJECTION: Remove eval/exec; use safe parsers or explicit logic.
